author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg: @AbongileViksVika @RickMiddleton even farms without sim could still have the SIM card holder module on the board but it would not be functional. What they do is testing all motherboards and keep the ones where the sim can function seperate. Then those are further debugged to make sure the sim works. The one with non functional SIM card holders will not be further debugged. So there is a difference. That said, I didn't get any reports from attestation errors yet, regardless the type of farm.

author: Jaba - Unityfarmers
msg: Maybe it's a matter of luck. But I didn't get a single person yet reporting it happened on their farms and there's quite a few around

author: Rick Middleton
msg:
Mine have dropped a little today..   it is actually lowest earning in 30 days for me - calibration killed all the marketplace ones I had ofc.

These are the actual phones I have with me here - on Starlink wifi.

0.5055 ( main Android with SIM but on wifi mostly )

These 7 are real phones at my mum's house:

0.2804
0.2485
0.2304
0.2164
0.2159
0.2298
0.1262 ( has a SIM in it but mostly on wifi )
Total = 1.5476


I have speculated before that for Telemetry there are diminishing returns when you run multiple tests on the same IP...  there is no reason why a client would pay a lot of times for tests on the same route..  a couple for validation yes.. but not too many.  Rewards seem to support that hypothesis..

I added the earnings up for fun - has anyone else done that?   Be interesting to see the totals people are getting per IP address

author: Dave
msg:
I agree with that hypothesis and actually tested it. When I carried some boxes to friends. I set it all up at my place: typical low rewards

Once everything was setup I brought them to family and friends often far away and in total rural areas.

The whole box was generating slowly decreasing rewards starting around 40c down to 10c over the span of a week. And then it went into nothingness...

It did 3 boxes during the calibration phase, all reacted the same.

So I did 1 try with my own boxes and completely switched ISP -> same behaviour but starting at 20c

author: Dave
msg:
I disagree with the customer stuff. I don't believe they have customers yet, but for S&R.

I believe this calibration is some kind of AI doing exactly this distribution strategy.

If they have seen your geolocation often -> lower rewards; if they have seen your IP often -> even lower rewards

author: Dave
msg: And they are feeding us some mint money back

author: Dave
msg: But ofc that is my opinion and I can't prove it.

author: Rick Middleton
msg:
Not sure what you mean by disagreement on customers for telemetry data?  Where else does the payment come from?

Wether customers were paying from the get go or wether Unity were subsiding our rewards makes no difference...  Nobody will pay for multiple telemetry tests...   If they don't pay - we don't get paid.  Simple as that.

author: Dave
msg:
yes. that is the general problem:
Nobody needs your data multiple times for a long time frame especially if it is stable data.

author: Dave
msg: and i agree, i dont mind where my reward comes from. I just dont believe it is "customer behaviour" like the team is stating it :-)

author: Rick Middleton
msg:
I think customer behavior is quite easy to see.... Or rather NOT see

That's why they did this whole Calibration thing.   People did not want to pay us to do tasks ( and I would guess that also includes CLI and SMS verification ) if they can see the data is synthetic shite.

author: Dave
msg: oh, yes. snythetic shit is dangerous. i also fully agree on that. If Emulators make their way into this it will destroy it.

author: Rick Middleton
msg:
There were many thousands of those... that was the main problem I think that started calibration.

Unfortunately we are kind of in the middle I think.  Our boxes are legit devices so should be good as long as we don't run too many in one place..  but techniques to find emulators might hit us too..  like this attestation thing

>10% above minimum is green
